completeness_goals.goal: Create a SQL migration that adds a nullable billing_processor_id VARCHAR column to the business_profile table (up.sql with ADD COLUMN, down.sql with DROP COLUMN)
completeness_goals.rationale: The task requires persisting a new field. The oracle creates migrations/…/up.sql with ALTER TABLE business_profile ADD COLUMN billing_processor_id VARCHAR(64) and down.sql with DROP COLUMN. Test gate G1 (0.15) verifies this.

completeness_goals.goal: Declare billing_processor_id as Nullable<Varchar> in the diesel schema definition for the business_profile table (schema.rs and/or schema_v2.rs)
completeness_goals.rationale: Diesel ORM requires schema declarations matching the DB. The oracle adds billing_processor_id -> Nullable<Varchar> in the business_profile table block of schema.rs and schema_v2.rs. Test gate G2 (0.10) verifies this.

completeness_goals.goal: Add billing_processor_id field to all three diesel model structs: the main Profile struct, ProfileNew (for inserts), and ProfileUpdateInternal (for updates) in the diesel models layer
completeness_goals.rationale: All three structs must carry the field for reads, inserts, and updates to work. The oracle adds pub billing_processor_id: Option<MerchantConnectorAccountId> to all three. Test gate G3 (0.20) verifies all three.

completeness_goals.goal: Thread billing_processor_id through the apply_changeset (or equivalent update-merge) function so that it is destructured from the update and merged into the resulting Profile using fallback-to-source semantics (e.g., .or(source.billing_processor_id))
completeness_goals.rationale: Without apply_changeset threading, partial updates would silently drop the field. The oracle destructures billing_processor_id and applies .or(source.billing_processor_id) in both v1 and v2 apply_changeset impls. Test gate G4 (0.20) verifies this.

completeness_goals.goal: Add billing_processor_id field (typed as Option<MerchantConnectorAccountId>) to the domain-level Profile struct and wire it through the domain model's conversion/setter logic
completeness_goals.rationale: The domain model is the canonical representation used by business logic. The oracle adds the field to domain Profile, ProfileSetter, ProfileGeneralUpdate, and the From/Conversion impls. Test gate G5 (0.15) verifies the domain struct carries the field with correct type.

completeness_goals.goal: Thread billing_processor_id through all Profile construction sites in the codebase (including event/test construction) so that no compilation errors remain from the new field
completeness_goals.rationale: Any struct literal constructing Profile must include billing_processor_id or the code won't compile. The oracle adds billing_processor_id: None in events.rs test construction. Test gate G6 (0.10) verifies events.rs threading.

completeness_goals.goal: Achieve broad completeness by touching at least 4 of the 5 critical layers: migration, diesel schema, diesel models, domain models, and events/construction sites
completeness_goals.rationale: A partial implementation that only touches 1-2 layers leaves the codebase in an inconsistent state. Test gate G7 (0.10) requires at least 4/5 critical files to mention billing_processor_id.

decomposition_notes: This is a single-turn task: add billing_processor_id to the business_profile across all layers of a Rust payment processing system. The task decomposes into 5 core goals (one per critical layer: migration, schema, diesel models, apply_changeset threading, domain models) and 2 secondary goals (event construction threading, cross-layer completeness). Using 5 cores + 2 secondaries: sum_mult = 5*4 + 2*1 = 22, each core = 4/22 ≈ 0.18, each sec = 1/22 ≈ 0.045. Rounded cores to 0.18 each (0.90 total) and secondaries to 0.05 each (0.10 total) to sum to 1.0. The test gates G1-G7 map directly to these goals. Goals G6 and G7 are secondary because they are derivative — if the 5 core layers are done correctly, events threading and completeness follow naturally.
